ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002187 covers doughnut food product as closest parent; no specific chocolate-frosted variant code exists in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers doughnuts as a category; no chocolate-frosted subtype code exists. Used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate frosted donut. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular commercial pastry codes.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate frosted donut as served at Dunkin' Donuts, approximately 57 g.

nutrient_source.title: Doughnuts, cake-type, chocolate-coated or frosted (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18249)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cake-type chocolate-coated/frosted doughnut used as primary reference. The Dunkin' Choc Frost is a yeast-raised ring donut with dark chocolate frosting; values adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 (cake-type chocolate-frosted doughnut) as the closest approved analogue. Yeast-raised donuts are slightly lower in fat and higher in carbohydrate than cake-type; values represent a reasonable central estimate. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Dunkin' Choc Frost specifically. GI of 76 is based on published GI values for cake-type and yeast-raised doughnuts reported in Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283), which lists doughnuts at GI ~76 (glucose reference). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (76 × 27.93) / 100 ≈ 21.3, using serving size 57 g and carbohydrate_g 49.0 per 100 g (49.0 × 0.57 = 27.93 g carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Each donut provides approximately 182 mg sodium; individuals managing hypertension should limit intake to occasional consumption and monitor total daily sodium from all sources, keeping within the recommended 2000 mg/day ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (76) and high glycemic load per serving (~21)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The high GI of 76 and glycemic load of approximately 21 per donut make this food unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, as it will produce a rapid and substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; it should be avoided or strictly limited to very small portions on rare occasions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: With approximately 5.1 g saturated fat and 0.1 g trans fat per donut, combined with 13.7 g of sugar, regular consumption of chocolate frosted donuts is associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chocolate Frosted Donut (marketed as 'Choc Frost' at Dunkin' Donuts Singapore) is a yeast-raised ring doughnut coated with a dark chocolate glaze or frosting. The dough is made from enriched wheat flour, sugar, yeast, eggs, and milk, deep-fried in oil until golden, then dipped or enrobed in a dark chocolate-flavoured icing that sets to a smooth, slightly firm coating. The result is a soft, airy, mildly sweet interior with a rich, bittersweet chocolate exterior. Texturally, the crumb is tender and slightly chewy, characteristic of yeast-leavened fried dough. Nutritionally, a single donut (approximately 57 g) provides around 223 kcal, 11.4 g fat (including approximately 5.1 g saturated fat), 27.9 g carbohydrate, and 13.7 g sugar. The glycemic index is high (approximately 76), making it a high-glycemic food that causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation. It is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing cardiovascular disease on a regular basis. The product is widely available at Dunkin' Donuts outlets across Singapore and is a popular snack or breakfast item. It is vegetarian and typically halal-certified in Singapore. Clinically, its combination of refined carbohydrates, saturated fat, and added sugar places it in the discretionary food category, recommended only for occasional consumption.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Dunkin Donuts chocolate frosted donut?

Is a chocolate frosted donut vegetarian?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data for Dunkin' Donuts Singapore Choc Frost is available from an approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for chocolate-frosted cake-type doughnuts (NDB 18249), which is the closest approved analogue. GI is estimated from published doughnut GI values in Atkinson et al. (2008). Confidence is medium due to product-type adaptation rather than exact match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 18249 (chocolate-frosted cake-type doughnut) as the closest approved analogue for this yeast-raised product; minor compositional differences between cake-type and yeast-raised donuts are acknowledged. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry used. 3. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 5. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 6. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 7. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 8. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source entry. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero as doughnuts contain no appreciable vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 10. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on Atkinson et al. (2008) doughnut GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database. 11. Halal status reflects Dunkin' Donuts Singapore halal certification; kosher status is not confirmed. 12. The food_name 'Choc Frost' from the input has been expanded to the canonical English name 'Chocolate Frosted Donut' for database clarity; 'Choc Frost' is retained as a primary alias.
